title: Piper mollipilosum
intro: Piper mollipilosum, also known as the many-spined pepper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential antimicrobial and anticancer activities. The bioactive constituents of Piper mollipilosum include alkaloids such as piperine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, with the most common herbal preparations being decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory infections.
